name: Fertilizer / Urea
summary: Day 63 Thursday. CF INDUSTRIES +3.7% 1D TO $126.78 — FERTILIZER FIRMING ON QATAR-FERTILIZER-EXPORT CONCERN + BRENT FEEDSTOCK COST-PASS-THROUGH; CF Q1 MAY 6 STILL THE BINARY TEST. CF $126.78 (+3.7% 1D, -7.9% 1M, +36.7% 3M) — strongest day of fertilizer trade in 2 weeks. Drivers: Brent $118-120 close (+6-8% 1D, Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED) elevates feedstock cost; Qatar fertilizer-export concern persists (downstream urea HALTED per Carnegie/Brownfield); UAE OPEC exit takes effect TOMORROW May 1 (Gulf realignment indirect-bullish for fertilizer-as-Hormuz-route-dependent). Nutrien +1.7%; Linde -1.1%; BASF data error today. The equity-vs-physical divergence persists: physical urea Yuzhny FOB ~$692/mt FLAT vs equity CF firming. CF Q1 May 6 IS THE BINARY TEST of equity-vs-realized-pricing divergence: physical urea $692/mt 1Q realized pricing → CF base case Q1 reflects strong realized pricing; May 6 print could be POSITIVE SURPRISE vs the de-escalation-discounted equity. QatarEnergy downstream urea HALTED structurally — physical supply chain unchanged 2027. Linde + BASF chemical/industrial-gas rotation intact at producer level via spot-premium capture. Industrial-gas-producer rotation captures helium spot premium ($1,000-1,200/Mcf vs $500 pre-war) without chip-margin pass-through (Samsung Q1 NO HELIUM MENTION confirms tier-1 falsification). PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): Tue: CF Industries $123.63 (+2.2% 1D, -6.7% 1M, +33.4% 3M); Nutrien $72.37 (+1.1%); Linde $510.75 (+0.1% 1D, +3.1% 1M, +13.5% 3M); Urea Yuzhny FOB ~$692.25/mt (Apr 24, -0.25% 1D, FLAT 1M, +60% YoY). Argus reported Middle East granular urea sold high-$410s/mt FOB recently — wide regional spreads. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
